comment.body:
So I'm working on the homework from yesterday (Heston-Nandi Monte Carlo), do you feed it new data every minute in order to have an accurate representation of the potential paths of vol or do you just run it at the beginning of the session? 

Also, it seems to differ from Heston models in that it's used for closed form European options, does that matter, or do you still use it for US options as well? I know there's a few key differences between the two so I'm wondering if that impacts the math behind the GARCH model and therefore the HN-MC. 

Also, do you use Heston's SV model at all or is there something better to use now?
comment.raw_body:
So I'm working on the homework from yesterday (Heston-Nandi Monte Carlo), do you feed it new data every minute in order to have an accurate representation of the potential paths of vol or do you just run it at the beginning of the session? 

Also, it seems to differ from Heston models in that it's used for closed form European options, does that matter, or do you still use it for US options as well? I know there's a few key differences between the two so I'm wondering if that impacts the math behind the GARCH model and therefore the HN-MC. 

Also, do you use Heston's SV model at all or is there something better to use now?
